message:
bd-1av0.8: fix node:http shim to use pi.http() + add 21 response-path tests

The http shim was referencing `globalThis.__pi_bridge.http` which doesn't
exist — the correct bridge object is `globalThis.pi.http`. This bug meant
all HTTP requests from extensions using node:http would always fail with
"HTTP requests require pi.http() hostcall" instead of routing through the
capability-gated hostcall system.

Fix: Change `_send()` to check `globalThis.pi.http` instead.

Add 21 new tests covering the full request → response flow via mocked
pi.http: GET/POST with body, URL construction, header normalization,
status codes, error handling (rejection, invalid response), event ordering
(data/end), abort/destroy, timeout forwarding, https protocol, and
end-with-chunk.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
